summary: Matched a block of Mario fighter code by moving several routines from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.s into src/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.c. The PR decompiled functions around func_800E0D1C through func_800E10EC, including Mario/Dr. Mario-style special/action setup, callbacks, random selection of x222C/x2230 variants via HSD_Randi, accessory callback func_800E0EE0, and effect/item-spawn calls. It also replaced two float sdata extern uses with direct 3.0f and 0.0f literals, removed unused lbl_804D9190/lbl_804D9194 data, and updated remaining assembly references from lbl_800E0EE0 to func_800E0EE0. Review caught that func_800E0D1C needed to return the selected value because Dr. Mario used that return path; the author accepted and made it return int.

key_files.role: Added matching C implementations for Mario fighter routines and helper logic; added random.h include and externs for fighter/effect callbacks.

Removed the now-decompiled assembly for func_800E0D1C, func_800E0DA8, func_800E0E18, func_800E0E54, func_800E0E84, func_800E0EA4, func_800E0EE0, func_800E1040, func_800E10B0, and func_800E10EC; kept later assembly and adjusted callback symbol references.

what_changed: Deleted about 300 lines of ftmario assembly and replaced them with C implementations in src/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.c.
Added #include <sysdolphin/baselib/random.h> to use HSD_Randi in C.

Added extern declarations for functions used by the newly decompiled code, including func_8008A4D4, func_800E0EE0, func_80084F3C, func_8007500C, func_8000B1CC, func_8029B6F8, func_802C0510, and ef_Spawn.
Added inline helper _func_800E0EE0_arr_copy(Fighter* ft_2, int* arr), which fills an array with values 0..8 excluding ft_2->x222C and ft_2->x2230 and returns the output count.
Implemented func_800E0D1C in C as a random variant selector: builds the filtered array, picks arr[HSD_Randi(outpos)], shifts x222C into x2230, writes the new x222C, and returns the selected value.

Implemented action/setup functions func_800E0DA8 and func_800E1040 that clear x2200_ftcmd_var0 and x2210_ThrowFlags.flags, call func_800693AC with action IDs 0x157/0x158, call func_8006EBA4, and assign ft->cb.x21BC_callback_Accessory4 = func_800E0EE0.

Implemented simple transition/interrupt wrappers: func_800E0E18 calls func_8008A2BC when func_8006F238 returns 0; func_800E0EA4 calls func_800E1178 when func_80082708 returns 0; func_800E10B0 calls func_800CC730 when func_8006F238 returns 0.
Implemented callback-gated routines func_800E0E54 and func_800E10EC, which call func_8008A4D4 or func_800CCAAC only when ft->x2200_ftcmd_var0 is nonzero.
Implemented func_800E0E84 as a direct wrapper around func_80084F3C.

Implemented func_800E0EE0 in C: checks and clears ft->x2210_ThrowFlags.b0, gets bone coordinates with func_8000B1CC and func_8007500C, branches on ft->x4_fighterKind, then either calls func_8029B6F8 plus ef_Spawn or chooses a random x222C/x2230 variant and calls func_802C0510.
Changed func_800E0C24 and func_800E0C68 to pass direct float literals 3.0f and 0.0f to func_800704F0 instead of extern constants lbl_804D9190 and lbl_804D9194.
Removed lbl_804D9190 and lbl_804D9194 from ftmario.s sdata2 and retained lbl_804D9198/lbl_804D919C for 0.0f/1.0f constants used by the new C code.
Updated remaining assembly callback-address loads in func_800E1178 and func_800E11E0 from lbl_800E0EE0 to func_800E0EE0.
smart_moves: Factored the repeated filtered-random-array loop into _func_800E0EE0_arr_copy for the larger callback while keeping func_800E0D1C’s version explicit enough to match its local stack/register behavior.
Preserved matching-sensitive locals and stack layout with explicit temporary variables and padding arrays, e.g. u8 padding[8], arr[9], arr[11], tmp/tmp2, and outsize.

Used project struct/field names where available instead of raw offsets for fighter state: x2200_ftcmd_var0, x2210_ThrowFlags.flags, x2210_ThrowFlags.b0, x222C, x2230, x5E8_fighterBones, x4_fighterKind, and cb.x21BC_callback_Accessory4.

Converted the internal assembly label lbl_800E0EE0 into callable C symbol func_800E0EE0 and updated remaining asm references, which avoids dangling label-style callback references after decompilation.
Removed unnecessary sdata float symbols by replacing them with direct float literals where the code was clearer and still matched.
decomp_lessons: A function that appears void from its local behavior can still need a return value because another character file or sibling implementation depends on it; review explicitly noted func_800E0D1C is used by Dr. Mario and should return r3.
When decompiling fighter callbacks, inspect sibling characters such as Dr. Mario for ABI expectations, return types, and reused helper semantics.
Avoid helper names containing 'nonmatching' if the function is actually part of a matching C solution; reviewers may read that as a decomp status marker rather than a semantic description.

For matching loops that build temporary arrays, the exact local array size, helper inlining, and padding can matter. This PR used arr[9]/arr[11], explicit outpos, and padding to align with generated code.
Callback bitfield handling can be expressed through named bitfields when the struct supports it; here x2210_ThrowFlags.b0 replaced the raw rlwinm/rlwimi assembly pattern.
After moving a label-defined assembly routine into C, remember to update any remaining assembly address loads to the function symbol name.
naming_conventions: Use func_XXXXXXXX for still-unnamed decompiled functions when no semantic name is established, e.g. func_800E0D1C and func_800E0EE0.
Use ftMario_OnDeath for the known Mario death callback while leaving unknown nearby routines in address-based names.
Helper _func_800E0EE0_arr_copy names the helper after the parent function and its array-copy role; this was chosen after a reviewer questioned a confusing earlier name containing 'copy_nonmatching'.

Avoid the word 'nonmatching' in helper names unless it refers to decompilation match status; in this PR discussion the author clarified that the old name meant values not matching attributes, but agreed it was confusing.
Use field names from Fighter rather than raw offsets where available, including x2200_ftcmd_var0, x2210_ThrowFlags, x222C, x2230, and x21BC_callback_Accessory4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used HSD_Randi(outpos/outsize) with a filtered local int array to reproduce the original random-selection assembly pattern.
Used explicit assignments ft->x2230 = ft->x222C followed by ft->x222C = selected_value to match the observed order of stores.
Preserved stack/register behavior with seemingly artificial locals: r3, i, outpos in func_800E0D1C; flag_res, tmp, tmp2, padding, and arr[11] in func_800E0EE0.
Kept func_800E0D1C’s return as the selected random value after review; reviewer suggested returning r3 would still match.
Expressed action-state setup calls with exact action IDs 0x157 and 0x158 and existing constants lbl_804D9198/lbl_804D919C for 0.0f/1.0f parameters.
Matched callback assignment by storing func_800E0EE0 into ft->cb.x21BC_callback_Accessory4 instead of leaving an asm local label.
Used direct float literals for 3.0f and 0.0f in func_800E0C24/func_800E0C68, allowing removal of lbl_804D9190/lbl_804D9194.
review_feedback: PsiLupan asked why a helper had a name like 'copy_nonmatching' despite being part of a matching function, flagging ambiguity between semantic 'nonmatching values' and decompilation match status.

nicolasrmerz replied that the name came from r-burns's decomp.me scratch and meant copying integers that do not match the fighter attributes in the conditional, not nonmatching decomp status, and said it would be renamed to reduce confusion.
r-burns reviewed src/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.c and noted that func_800E0D1C needed to return something because it is used by Dr. Mario; he suggested `return r3` should still match.
nicolasrmerz acknowledged the missing return-value catch.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or semantically identify func_800E0D1C or func_800E0EE0.
Search Dr. Mario files for func_800E0D1C usage to confirm the cross-character return-value dependency noted in review.
Look for subsequent cleanup of extern declarations in ftmario.c into headers.
Search for other fighter PRs that update assembly callback labels from lbl_XXXXXXXX to func_XXXXXXXX after decompiling the callback.
Find other uses of x222C/x2230 random-selection helpers to compare naming and matching patterns.
